refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing operational efficiency and resource utilization.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; refining it into sub-FRs focusing on key productivity drivers (operational efficiency and resource utilization) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and verify that operational efficiency and resource utilization metrics meet targets.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize operational efficiency to maximize productive output.
FR2: Optimize resource utilization to minimize waste and downtime.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement real-time operational monitoring and adaptive control algorithms.
DP2: Integrate predictive maintenance and resource scheduling systems.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement real-time operational monitoring and adaptive control algorithms.

coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses FR1 with minimal coupling to FR2, enabling modular design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate real-time telemetry data.
Risk that adaptive control algorithms may require integration with safety services due to high safety criticality.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding explicit interface requirements with Safety Services to mitigate safety risks.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded control systems responsible for real-time operational monitoring and adaptive control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data exchange for real-time monitoring and control feedback.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Output alerts to Safety Services in case of detected operational anomalies.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope, less focused containment for this DP.
High containment score due to embedded control focus; low diffusion risk.
Partial containment possible but less optimal due to focus on remote control.
Primarily for safety alert handling, not operational control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Vehicle module centralizes real-time control logic, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
Risk of overloading Vehicle module with both control and safety responsibilities.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Safety Services and consider modularizing safety alert logic.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP1 directly addresses the refined FR1 with clear logical mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion of safety alert logic between Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for DP1, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and operational performance metrics collected from Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of DP1 to Vehicle module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface definitions between Vehicle and Safety Services for safety alert handling.
Consider explicit FR and DP refinement for safety alert generation.

decision.justification: While the FR to DP and DP to IM mappings are sound, safety alert handling requires clearer functional decomposition and interface definition to address high safety criticality.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize operational efficiency to maximize productive output.
FR2: Optimize resource utilization to minimize waste and downtime.
FR3: Ensure safety alert generation and communication during operational anomalies.

revise.why: To explicitly include safety alert generation as a functional requirement, enabling clearer DP and IM mapping.

assumptions: Telemetry data is accurate and timely.
Vehicle module can support real-time control and alert generation.
open_risks: Safety alert logic diffusion between Vehicle and Safety Services.
Potential integration complexity with Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data quality and latency analysis.
Interface specification between Vehicle and Safety Services.
Verification plan for safety alert generation.
